differential_diagnoses: Acute hypertensive crisis with hypertensive encephalopathy
Metabolic encephalopathy (e.g., severe anemia or electrolyte disturbance)
Cerebral venous sinus thrombosis (missed by initial imaging)
next_steps: Initiate titration of blood pressure to achieve gradual, controlled reduction using intravenous agents (e.g., clevidipine or labetalol)
Start antiepileptic therapy (e.g., levetiracetam) for seizure prophylaxis
Obtain serum electrolytes including magnesium, calcium, and phosphorus
Evaluate renal function and screen for proteinuria to assess for potential nephropathy-induced hypertension
Perform a comprehensive hematologic workup (LDH, haptoglobin, reticulocyte count) to assess for ongoing hyperhemolysis
Continuous neurological monitoring and monitoring for further seizure activity
Consider repeat neuroimaging (e.g., MRV) if neurological status fails to stabilize, to definitively rule out venous sinus thrombosis
